revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)}}. Komaro’s final rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)}}. Komaro’s final rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)}}. Komaro’s final rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. == Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Asuka period people]] [[Category:People of Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Taihō Code]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:710 deaths]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]] [[ja:下毛野古麻呂]] [[en:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno no Komaro}}

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|12=draft}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=draft}}. Komaro’s final rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国|12=draft}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡|12=draft}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|12=draft}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|12=draft}}. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|12=draft}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022. <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro-->

== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022. <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro-->

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing imported pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|12=draft}}, who held the rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=draft}}. Komaro’s final rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国|12=draft}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡|12=draft}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡|12=draft}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|12=draft}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|12=draft}}. At the time, he held the rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|12=draft}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022. <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro-->

In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft|12=draft}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft|12=draft}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=draft|12=draft}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft|12=draft}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|12=draft|12=draft}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft|12=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft|12=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=draft|12=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|12=draft|12=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=draft|12=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|12=draft|12=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft|12=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft|12=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=draft|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|12=draft|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

[[sn:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]] [[ja:下毛野古麻呂]] [[en:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]]

<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro-->

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 下毛野 古麻呂 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]]後期 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[和銅]]2年[[12月20日 (旧暦)|12月20日]]<ref>『続日本紀』による。『公卿補任』では12月16日とする。</ref>（[[710年]][[1月28日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 子麻呂<ref>『日本古代人名辭典』第四巻p.901。『日本古代氏族人名辞典』p.343。『日本書紀』[[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）[[10月22日 (旧暦)|10月22日]]条。</ref> | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正四位|正四位下]]・[[参議]] | 主君 = [[持統天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|持統天皇}}]]→[[文武天皇]]→[[元明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}]] | 氏族 = [[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]→[[朝臣]] | 父母 = 父：下毛野久志麻呂 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = [[下野国]][[河内郡]]の[[下野薬師寺跡|下野薬師寺]]の建立者。 }}
'''下毛野 古麻呂'''（しもつけの の こまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]後期の[[公卿]]。名は'''子麻呂'''とも記される。[[カバネ|姓]]は君のち[[朝臣]]。[[大義 (冠位)|大義冠]]・下毛野久志麻呂の子。[[官位]]は[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[参議]]。
== 出自 == 下毛野古麻呂は[[下毛野国造]]家である[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]を出自とする[[貴族]]で、[[下野国]][[河内郡]]を本拠地とした。下野河内郡に[[下野薬師寺跡|下野薬師寺]]を建立し[[氏寺]]としたと云われる。
== 経歴 == [[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）[[奴婢]]600人の解放を[[上奏]]して許可される（この時の[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位]]は直廣肆）<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇3年10月22日条</ref>。
[[文武天皇]]4年（[[700年]]）[[文武天皇]]により、[[忍壁皇子|忍壁親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らと共に、[[律令]]の選定を命じられる（この時の冠位は直廣参）<ref>『続日本紀』文武天皇4年6月17日</ref>。翌[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）大宝律令による[[位階]]制の施行により[[従四位|従四位下]]に叙せられると、4月に古麻呂ら3人が初めて新令を講義して親王諸臣百官人に学ばせ<ref>『続日本紀』大宝元年4月7日条</ref>、同年8月には[[大宝律令]]を完成させている<ref>『続日本紀』大宝元年8月3日条</ref>。大宝律令選定の功労により、大宝2年（[[702年]]）[[参議]]に任ぜられ、大宝3年（[[703年]]）には[[功田]]30町と[[封戸]]50戸を与えられている。
その後文武朝では、[[兵部省|兵部卿]]を兼ねて従四位上まで昇進し、[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）の文武天皇の[[崩御]]に際しては、造山陵司を務める。また、同年には一族の[[下毛野石代]]に対して下毛野朝臣姓から下毛野川内朝臣姓への[[改姓]]を請い、許されている<ref>『続日本紀』慶雲4年3月22日条</ref>。
[[元明天皇|元明朝]]の[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）[[正四位|正四位下]]・[[式部省|式部卿]]に叙任され、大将軍も兼ねるが、和銅2年（[[709年]]）12月20日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。最終[[官位]]は参議式部卿大将軍正四位下。なお、古麻呂の卒去により[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]）[[藤原房前]]が任ぜられるまでの間8年間に亘って、参議が不在の状況となっている<ref>『公卿補任』</ref>。
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 * [[持統天皇]]3年（[[689年]]）以前：直廣肆（[[従五位|従五位下]]に相当） * [[文武天皇]]4年（[[700年]]）以前：直廣参（[[正五位|正五位下]]に相当） * [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]） 日付不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]]。4月7日：見[[弁官|右大弁]] * 大宝2年（[[702年]]） 5月21日：[[参議]] * 大宝3年（[[703年]]） 2月15日：[[功田]]10町、[[封戸]]50戸。3月7日：功田20町 * [[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]） 4月20日：従四位上<ref>『公卿補任』。また同日に兵部卿に任官しとする。</ref>。4月22日：兼[[兵部省|兵部卿]] * 慶雲4年（[[707年]]） 10月3日：造山陵司（[[文武天皇]]崩御） * [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） 3月13日：[[式部省|式部卿]]。7月1日：大将軍<ref>『公卿補任』。また同日に正四位下に叙せられたとする。</ref>。7月15日：[[正四位|正四位下]] * 和銅2年（[[709年]]） [[12月20日 (旧暦)|12月20日]]：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]（参議式部卿大将軍正四位下）
== その他 == * 2016年、[[小山市|小山]]北[[ロータリークラブ]]によって下毛野古麻呂の歴史漫画が作られた<ref>読売新聞栃木版 2016年4月14日 30面掲載。</ref>。2017年、その歴史漫画が動画化された<ref>読売新聞栃木版 2017年2月19日 34面掲載。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年、ISBN 4061590308 * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[吉川弘文館]]、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 * [[竹内理三]]他編『日本古代人名辭典』第四巻、吉川弘文館、1963年、ISBN 4642020047 * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82] 無料公開マンガふるさとの偉人「下毛野古麻呂」 発行 栃木県下野市教育委員会事務局文化財課 2022年3月
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の こまろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|こまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:下野国の人物]] [[Category:大宝律令]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:710年没]]

{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の こまろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|こまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:下野国の人物]] [[Category:大宝律令]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:710年没]]

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro-->

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō||ja|下毛野国造}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja||ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja||ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja||ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja|ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi District|qq=}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushiji|qq=}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osakabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fuhito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Mahito|qq=}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō|qq=}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fusasaki|qq=}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six National Histories|qq=}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishogun (directional deity)|lt=Daishogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishogun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Risan|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}}.

His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja|ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi District|qq=}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushiji|qq=}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osakabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fuhito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Mahito|qq=}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō|qq=}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fusasaki|qq=}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six National Histories|qq=}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Risan|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.
