revision.comment: Text replacement - "|参議|" to "|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|"
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja|ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi District|qq=}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushiji|qq=}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osakabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fuhito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Mahito|qq=}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō|qq=}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fusasaki|qq=}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six National Histories|qq=}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Risan|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fusasaki|qq=}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six National Histories|qq=}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Risan|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]]
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}}.
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}, specifically in the {{ill|Kawachi District|ja|ja|河内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi District|qq=}}. He is believed to have founded {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushiji|ja|ja|下野薬師寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushiji|qq=}}, which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|ja|忍壁皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osakabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|ja|ja|藤原不比等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fuhito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Awata no Mahito|ja|粟田真人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata no Mahito|qq=}}, to draft the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō|qq=}}. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|ja|ja|藤原房前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Fusasaki|qq=}} assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six National Histories|qq=}}: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangi|qq=}} * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * {{ill|Takeuchi Risan|ja|竹内理三|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Risan|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:下毛野氏
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:People of Asuka-period Japan
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.
