<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> [[Category:下毛野氏]] [[Category:People of Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

[[sn:Shimotsukeno no Komaro]] [[ja:下毛野古麻呂]]
<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> [[Category:下毛野氏]] [[Category:People of Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]]

'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>

== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:People of Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>

== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:People of Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>

== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[:en:slaves|slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)

<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:People of Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]]

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0710|1|28}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|title={{ill|Sangi|qid=Q1133663}}; {{ill|taishōgun|qid=Q5369529}}|family={{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|qid=Q16142940}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|qq=|1=Daigi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[:en:slaves|slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=|1=Rotary Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276992}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>

During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|qq=|1=Rotary Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276992}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
