The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}} gives the 16th day of the 12th month.</ref> (28 January 710) | 改名 = | 別名 = Komaro<ref>{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Personal Names|ja|日本古代人名辭典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV p.901; {{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} p.343; [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], 22 X 689 (Jitō 3).</ref> | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = Senior Fourth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shōshi-i|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123338}}) • [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] (Councillor) | 主君 = [[:en:Empress Jitō|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|持統天皇}}}}→[[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]→[[:en:Empress Genmei|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}}} | 氏族 = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}→[[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] | 父母 = Father: Shimotsuke no Kushimaro | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = Founder of [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District, Shimotsuke Province]] (present-day [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). }}
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' ({{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野 古麻呂|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was a [[:en:Kugyō|kugyō]] (court noble) of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also written '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His hereditary title ([[:en:Kabane|kabane]]) was originally {{ill|kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}, later [[:en:Ason|ason (kabane)]]. He was the son of Shimotsuke no Kushimaro, bearer of the {{ill|Taigi cap|ja|大義 (冠位)|1=Taigi cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro attained the rank of Senior Fourth Rank, Lower and served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] (Councillor).
== Lineage ==
Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, descendants of the {{ill|Shimotsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} line, whose seat was in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He is said to have built [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] there as the clan temple ({{ill|uji-dera|ja|氏寺|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}).
== Career ==
In 689 (Jitō 3) Komaro petitioned for the manumission of 600 [[:en:Nobi|Nuhi]] (slaves), which was granted; he then held the cap rank {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Jitō 3, 10 / 22.</ref>
In 700 (Monmu 4) Emperor Monmu ordered Komaro, [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]] and others to revise the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|ritsuryō codes]]; Komaro then held {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Monmu 4, 6 / 17.</ref>
With the promulgation of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 Komaro rose to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower. On 7 April he and two colleagues gave the first official lectures on the new code to princes and officials,<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 4 / 7.</ref> and by 3 August the code was completed.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 8 / 3.</ref> For this achievement he was made [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] in 702 and, in 703, received 30 chō of {{ill|meritorious fields|ja|功田|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}} and 50 fief households ({{ill|fuko|ja|封戸|1=fuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458391}}).
Thereafter he served concurrently as {{ill|Minister of War|ja|兵部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}, rising to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper. At Emperor Monmu’s death in 707 (Keiun 4) he was appointed {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervisor of the imperial mausoleum, and secured imperial approval for {{ill|Shimotsuke no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|1=Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361051}} to change the clan surname to Shimotsuke no Kawachi Ason.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Keiun 4, 3 / 22.</ref>
Under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] he became {{ill|Minister of Civil Affairs|ja|式部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Great General|ja|大将軍|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} in 708 (Wadō 1), being promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower the same year. He died on 20 December 709 (Wadō 2). His last offices were Councillor, Minister of Civil Affairs and Great General. The sangi post then remained vacant until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] was appointed in 717 (Reiki 3).<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref>
== Offices and Ranks ==
(From the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]].)
* by 689 — {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower) * by 700 — {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower) * 701 — Junior Fourth Rank, Lower 7 Apr 701 — {{ill|Udaiben|ja|右大弁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (Right Greater Controller) * 21 May 702 — [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * 15 Feb 703 — 10 chō meritorious fields 7 Mar 703 — 20 chō additional meritorious fields * 20 Apr 705 — Junior Fourth Rank, Upper<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 22 Apr 705 — Minister of War * 3 Oct 707 — {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (imperial tomb works) * 13 Mar 708 — Minister of Civil Affairs 1 Jul 708 — Great General<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 15 Jul 708 — Senior Fourth Rank, Lower * 20 Dec 709 — deceased (Councillor • Minister of Civil Affairs • Great General • Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Miscellaneous ==
* In 2016 the north-[[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama Rotary Club]] produced a historical manga about Komaro.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 14 Apr 2016, p.30.</ref> In 2017 the manga was adapted into an animated video.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 19 Feb 2017, p.34.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0710|1|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|title={{ill|Sangi|WD=Q1133663}}; {{ill|taishōgun|WD=Q5369529}}|family={{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|WD=Q16142940}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|1=Daigi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[:en:slaves|slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|1=Rotary Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276992}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)

The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}} gives the 16th day of the 12th month.</ref> (28 January 710) | 改名 = | 別名 = Komaro<ref>{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Personal Names|ja|日本古代人名辭典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV p.901; {{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} p.343; [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], 22 X 689 (Jitō 3).</ref> | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = Senior Fourth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shōshi-i|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123338}}) • [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] (Councillor) | 主君 = [[:en:Empress Jitō|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|持統天皇}}}}→[[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]→[[:en:Empress Genmei|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}}} | 氏族 = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}→[[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] | 父母 = Father: Shimotsuke no Kushimaro | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = Founder of [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District, Shimotsuke Province]] (present-day [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). }}
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' ({{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野 古麻呂|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was a [[:en:Kugyō|kugyō]] (court noble) of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also written '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His hereditary title ([[:en:Kabane|kabane]]) was originally {{ill|kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}, later [[:en:Ason|ason (kabane)]]. He was the son of Shimotsuke no Kushimaro, bearer of the {{ill|Taigi cap|ja|大義 (冠位)|1=Taigi cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro attained the rank of Senior Fourth Rank, Lower and served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] (Councillor).
== Lineage ==
Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, descendants of the {{ill|Shimotsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} line, whose seat was in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He is said to have built [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] there as the clan temple ({{ill|uji-dera|ja|氏寺|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}).
== Career ==
In 689 (Jitō 3) Komaro petitioned for the manumission of 600 [[:en:Nobi|Nuhi]] (slaves), which was granted; he then held the cap rank {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Jitō 3, 10 / 22.</ref>
In 700 (Monmu 4) Emperor Monmu ordered Komaro, [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]] and others to revise the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|ritsuryō codes]]; Komaro then held {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Monmu 4, 6 / 17.</ref>
With the promulgation of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 Komaro rose to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower. On 7 April he and two colleagues gave the first official lectures on the new code to princes and officials,<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 4 / 7.</ref> and by 3 August the code was completed.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 8 / 3.</ref> For this achievement he was made [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] in 702 and, in 703, received 30 chō of {{ill|meritorious fields|ja|功田|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}} and 50 fief households ({{ill|fuko|ja|封戸|1=fuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458391}}).
Thereafter he served concurrently as {{ill|Minister of War|ja|兵部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}, rising to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper. At Emperor Monmu’s death in 707 (Keiun 4) he was appointed {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervisor of the imperial mausoleum, and secured imperial approval for {{ill|Shimotsuke no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|1=Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361051}} to change the clan surname to Shimotsuke no Kawachi Ason.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Keiun 4, 3 / 22.</ref>
Under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] he became {{ill|Minister of Civil Affairs|ja|式部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Great General|ja|大将軍|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} in 708 (Wadō 1), being promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower the same year. He died on 20 December 709 (Wadō 2). His last offices were Councillor, Minister of Civil Affairs and Great General. The sangi post then remained vacant until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] was appointed in 717 (Reiki 3).<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref>
== Offices and Ranks ==
(From the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]].)
* by 689 — {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower) * by 700 — {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower) * 701 — Junior Fourth Rank, Lower 7 Apr 701 — {{ill|Udaiben|ja|右大弁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (Right Greater Controller) * 21 May 702 — [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * 15 Feb 703 — 10 chō meritorious fields 7 Mar 703 — 20 chō additional meritorious fields * 20 Apr 705 — Junior Fourth Rank, Upper<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 22 Apr 705 — Minister of War * 3 Oct 707 — {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (imperial tomb works) * 13 Mar 708 — Minister of Civil Affairs 1 Jul 708 — Great General<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 15 Jul 708 — Senior Fourth Rank, Lower * 20 Dec 709 — deceased (Councillor • Minister of Civil Affairs • Great General • Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Miscellaneous ==
* In 2016 the north-[[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama Rotary Club]] produced a historical manga about Komaro.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 14 Apr 2016, p.30.</ref> In 2017 the manga was adapted into an animated video.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 19 Feb 2017, p.34.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0710|1|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|title={{ill|Sangi|WD=Q1133663}}; {{ill|taishōgun|WD=Q5369529}}|family={{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|WD=Q16142940}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|1=Daigi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[:en:slaves|slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|1=Rotary Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276992}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.

<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = Shimotsukeno no Komaro | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = Late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | 生誕 = Unknown | 死没 = [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] 2 (old calendar) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日 (旧暦)|1=20th day of the 12th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841057}}<ref>According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}} gives the 16th day of the 12th month.</ref> (28 January 710) | 改名 = | 別名 = Komaro<ref>{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Personal Names|ja|日本古代人名辭典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV p.901; {{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} p.343; [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], 22 X 689 (Jitō 3).</ref> | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = Senior Fourth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shōshi-i|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123338}}) • [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] (Councillor) | 主君 = [[:en:Empress Jitō|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|持統天皇}}}}→[[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]→[[:en:Empress Genmei|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}}} | 氏族 = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}→[[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] | 父母 = Father: Shimotsuke no Kushimaro | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = Founder of [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District, Shimotsuke Province]] (present-day [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). }}
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' ({{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野 古麻呂|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was a [[:en:Kugyō|kugyō]] (court noble) of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also written '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His hereditary title ([[:en:Kabane|kabane]]) was originally {{ill|kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}, later [[:en:Ason|ason (kabane)]]. He was the son of Shimotsuke no Kushimaro, bearer of the {{ill|Taigi cap|ja|大義 (冠位)|1=Taigi cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro attained the rank of Senior Fourth Rank, Lower and served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] (Councillor).
== Lineage ==
Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, descendants of the {{ill|Shimotsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} line, whose seat was in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He is said to have built [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] there as the clan temple ({{ill|uji-dera|ja|氏寺|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}).
== Career ==
In 689 (Jitō 3) Komaro petitioned for the manumission of 600 [[:en:Nobi|Nuhi]] (slaves), which was granted; he then held the cap rank {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Jitō 3, 10 / 22.</ref>
In 700 (Monmu 4) Emperor Monmu ordered Komaro, [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]] and others to revise the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|ritsuryō codes]]; Komaro then held {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Monmu 4, 6 / 17.</ref>
With the promulgation of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 Komaro rose to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower. On 7 April he and two colleagues gave the first official lectures on the new code to princes and officials,<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 4 / 7.</ref> and by 3 August the code was completed.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 8 / 3.</ref> For this achievement he was made [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] in 702 and, in 703, received 30 chō of {{ill|meritorious fields|ja|功田|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}} and 50 fief households ({{ill|fuko|ja|封戸|1=fuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458391}}).
Thereafter he served concurrently as {{ill|Minister of War|ja|兵部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}, rising to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper. At Emperor Monmu’s death in 707 (Keiun 4) he was appointed {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervisor of the imperial mausoleum, and secured imperial approval for {{ill|Shimotsuke no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|1=Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361051}} to change the clan surname to Shimotsuke no Kawachi Ason.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Keiun 4, 3 / 22.</ref>
Under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] he became {{ill|Minister of Civil Affairs|ja|式部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Great General|ja|大将軍|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} in 708 (Wadō 1), being promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower the same year. He died on 20 December 709 (Wadō 2). His last offices were Councillor, Minister of Civil Affairs and Great General. The sangi post then remained vacant until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] was appointed in 717 (Reiki 3).<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref>
== Offices and Ranks ==
(From the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]].)
* by 689 — {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower) * by 700 — {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower) * 701 — Junior Fourth Rank, Lower 7 Apr 701 — {{ill|Udaiben|ja|右大弁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (Right Greater Controller) * 21 May 702 — [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * 15 Feb 703 — 10 chō meritorious fields 7 Mar 703 — 20 chō additional meritorious fields * 20 Apr 705 — Junior Fourth Rank, Upper<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 22 Apr 705 — Minister of War * 3 Oct 707 — {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (imperial tomb works) * 13 Mar 708 — Minister of Civil Affairs 1 Jul 708 — Great General<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 15 Jul 708 — Senior Fourth Rank, Lower * 20 Dec 709 — deceased (Councillor • Minister of Civil Affairs • Great General • Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Miscellaneous ==
* In 2016 the north-[[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama Rotary Club]] produced a historical manga about Komaro.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 14 Apr 2016, p.30.</ref> In 2017 the manga was adapted into an animated video.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 19 Feb 2017, p.34.</ref>
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}

''Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan''. {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1990. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0710|1|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|title={{ill|Sangi|WD=Q1133663}}; {{ill|taishōgun|WD=Q5369529}}|family={{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|WD=Q16142940}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|1=Daigi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[:en:slaves|slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|1=Rotary Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276992}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)

The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}} gives the 16th day of the 12th month.</ref> (28 January 710) | 改名 = | 別名 = Komaro<ref>{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Personal Names|ja|日本古代人名辭典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV p.901; {{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} p.343; [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], 22 X 689 (Jitō 3).</ref> | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = Senior Fourth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shōshi-i|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123338}}) • [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] (Councillor) | 主君 = [[:en:Empress Jitō|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|持統天皇}}}}→[[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]→[[:en:Empress Genmei|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}}} | 氏族 = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}→[[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] | 父母 = Father: Shimotsuke no Kushimaro | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = Founder of [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District, Shimotsuke Province]] (present-day [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). }}
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' ({{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野 古麻呂|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was a [[:en:Kugyō|kugyō]] (court noble) of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also written '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His hereditary title ([[:en:Kabane|kabane]]) was originally {{ill|kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}, later [[:en:Ason|ason (kabane)]]. He was the son of Shimotsuke no Kushimaro, bearer of the {{ill|Taigi cap|ja|大義 (冠位)|1=Taigi cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro attained the rank of Senior Fourth Rank, Lower and served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] (Councillor).
== Lineage ==
Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, descendants of the {{ill|Shimotsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} line, whose seat was in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He is said to have built [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] there as the clan temple ({{ill|uji-dera|ja|氏寺|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}).
== Career ==
In 689 (Jitō 3) Komaro petitioned for the manumission of 600 [[:en:Nobi|Nuhi]] (slaves), which was granted; he then held the cap rank {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Jitō 3, 10 / 22.</ref>
In 700 (Monmu 4) Emperor Monmu ordered Komaro, [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]] and others to revise the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|ritsuryō codes]]; Komaro then held {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Monmu 4, 6 / 17.</ref>
With the promulgation of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 Komaro rose to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower. On 7 April he and two colleagues gave the first official lectures on the new code to princes and officials,<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 4 / 7.</ref> and by 3 August the code was completed.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 8 / 3.</ref> For this achievement he was made [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] in 702 and, in 703, received 30 chō of {{ill|meritorious fields|ja|功田|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}} and 50 fief households ({{ill|fuko|ja|封戸|1=fuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458391}}).
Thereafter he served concurrently as {{ill|Minister of War|ja|兵部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}, rising to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper. At Emperor Monmu’s death in 707 (Keiun 4) he was appointed {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervisor of the imperial mausoleum, and secured imperial approval for {{ill|Shimotsuke no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|1=Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361051}} to change the clan surname to Shimotsuke no Kawachi Ason.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Keiun 4, 3 / 22.</ref>
Under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] he became {{ill|Minister of Civil Affairs|ja|式部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Great General|ja|大将軍|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} in 708 (Wadō 1), being promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower the same year. He died on 20 December 709 (Wadō 2). His last offices were Councillor, Minister of Civil Affairs and Great General. The sangi post then remained vacant until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] was appointed in 717 (Reiki 3).<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref>
== Offices and Ranks ==
(From the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]].)
* by 689 — {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower) * by 700 — {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower) * 701 — Junior Fourth Rank, Lower 7 Apr 701 — {{ill|Udaiben|ja|右大弁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (Right Greater Controller) * 21 May 702 — [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * 15 Feb 703 — 10 chō meritorious fields 7 Mar 703 — 20 chō additional meritorious fields * 20 Apr 705 — Junior Fourth Rank, Upper<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 22 Apr 705 — Minister of War * 3 Oct 707 — {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (imperial tomb works) * 13 Mar 708 — Minister of Civil Affairs 1 Jul 708 — Great General<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 15 Jul 708 — Senior Fourth Rank, Lower * 20 Dec 709 — deceased (Councillor • Minister of Civil Affairs • Great General • Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Miscellaneous ==
* In 2016 the north-[[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama Rotary Club]] produced a historical manga about Komaro.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 14 Apr 2016, p.30.</ref> In 2017 the manga was adapted into an animated video.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 19 Feb 2017, p.34.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]][[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0710|1|28}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|title={{ill|Sangi|WD=Q1133663}}; {{ill|taishōgun|WD=Q5369529}}|family={{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|WD=Q16142940}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' (下毛野 古麻呂) was a noble of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His [[kabane]] was initially ''Kimi'' and later ''Asomi''. He was the son of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja|下毛野久志麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kusimaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who held the Rank of {{ill|Daigi|ja|大義 (冠位)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigi|1=Daigi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro’s final Rank was {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]].
== Origins == Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, which originated from the {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kokuzō|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} lineage. His base of operations was in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], specifically in the [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]]. He is believed to have founded [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushiji]], which became the clan's temple.
== Career == In the third year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]’s reign (689), Komaro successfully petitioned to free 600 [[:en:slaves|slaves]], an action approved by the court. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Empress Jitō, 3rd year, 10th month, 22nd day.</ref>
In the fourth year of [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]'s reign (700), Komaro was appointed, along with [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], and [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]], to draft the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]. At the time, he held the Rank of {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Emperor Monmu, 4th year, 6th month, 17th day.</ref> The following year (701), after the enforcement of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]], he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}. In April of the same year, Komaro and two others lectured on the new laws to the court officials.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 4th month, 7th day.</ref> By August, the Taihō Code was completed.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 1st year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref> In recognition of his contributions, Komaro was appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] in 702 and was later granted 30 ''kōden'' (fields for merit) and 50 ''fuko'' (households for support).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Taihō 3rd year, 2nd month, 15th day and 3rd month, 7th day.</ref>
Under Emperor Monmu, Komaro served as {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} while being promoted to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in 705.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin,'' Monmu 4th year, 4th month, 20th day.</ref> In 707, following the emperor’s death, Komaro was appointed to oversee the construction of the imperial mausoleum.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 10th month, 3rd day.</ref> Additionally, he requested a name change for his relative {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|qq=}}, granting permission to adopt the surname {{ill|Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja|下毛野川内朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Keiun 4th year, 3rd month, 22nd day.</ref>
During the [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s reign in 708, Komaro was appointed {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and later {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}}. However, he passed away in December 709.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Wado 2nd year, 12th month, 20th day.</ref> Following his death, there were no Sangi appointments for eight years until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] assumed the role in 717.<ref>''Kugyō Bunin.''</ref>
== Legacy == In 2016, the history of Shimotsukeno no Komaro was adapted into a manga by the Oyamamachi North {{ill|Rotary Club|ja|ロータリークラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rotary Club|1=Rotary Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276992}}.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, April 14, 2016.</ref> In 2017, the manga was converted into a video format.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' Tochigi edition, February 19, 2017.</ref>
== Official Titles == According to the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]: * [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]], 3rd year (689): {{ill|Chokushishi|ja|直広肆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]], 4th year (700): {{ill|Chokushisan|ja|直広参|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokushisan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]], 1st year (701): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Right {{ill|Ōben|ja|大弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōben|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} * Taihō, 2nd year (702): [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * Taihō, 3rd year (703): Merit fields (10 ''kōden'' and 20 ''kōden'') * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]], 2nd year (705): {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, concurrently appointed {{ill|Hyōbu-kyō|ja|兵部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōbu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}} * Keiun, 4th year (707): Overseer of the Imperial Mausoleum * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]], 1st year (708): {{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}}, {{ill|Taishōgun|ja|大将軍|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)|lt=Daishōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishōgun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishōgun (directional deity)|qq=}} * Wadō, 2nd year (709): Passed away (Sangi, Shikibu-kyō, Taishōgun)
== References == * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Vol. 1),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, supervised by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names,'' [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. * [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Takeuchi Risan]], ''Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Names (Vol. 4),'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1963, ISBN 4642020047. * [https://www.bgf.or.jp/bgmanga/?s=%E4%B8%8B%E6%AF%9B%E9%87%8E%E5%8F%A4%E9%BA%BB%E5%91%82 Free Manga: "Shimotsukeno no Komaro"], Published by the Shimotsuke City Board of Education, March 2022.
{{translated page|ja|下毛野古麻呂|version=100367104|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:下毛野古麻呂]]
<!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Komaro--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:下毛野古麻呂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361047]] Wikidata name: Shimotsukeno no Komaro [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = Shimotsukeno no Komaro | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = Late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] | 生誕 = Unknown | 死没 = [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō]] 2 (old calendar) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日 (旧暦)|1=20th day of the 12th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q841057}}<ref>According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]. The {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}} gives the 16th day of the 12th month.</ref> (28 January 710) | 改名 = | 別名 = Komaro<ref>{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Personal Names|ja|日本古代人名辭典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} IV p.901; {{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} p.343; [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], 22 X 689 (Jitō 3).</ref> | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = Senior Fourth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Shōshi-i|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11123338}}) • [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] (Councillor) | 主君 = [[:en:Empress Jitō|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|持統天皇}}}}→[[:en:Emperor Monmu|Emperor Monmu]]→[[:en:Empress Genmei|{{color]]|lt_ja={{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}}} | 氏族 = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}→[[:en:Ason|Ason (kabane)]] | 父母 = Father: Shimotsuke no Kushimaro | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = Founder of [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District, Shimotsuke Province]] (present-day [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]). }}
'''Shimotsukeno no Komaro''' ({{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野 古麻呂|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was a [[:en:Kugyō|kugyō]] (court noble) of the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His name is also written '''Komaro''' (子麻呂). His hereditary title ([[:en:Kabane|kabane]]) was originally {{ill|kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}, later [[:en:Ason|ason (kabane)]]. He was the son of Shimotsuke no Kushimaro, bearer of the {{ill|Taigi cap|ja|大義 (冠位)|1=Taigi cap|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438510}}. Komaro attained the rank of Senior Fourth Rank, Lower and served as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] (Councillor).
== Lineage ==
Shimotsukeno no Komaro belonged to the {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsuke clan|ja|下毛野氏}}, descendants of the {{ill|Shimotsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}} line, whose seat was in [[:en:Kawachi District, Tochigi|Kawachi District]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. He is said to have built [[:en:Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] there as the clan temple ({{ill|uji-dera|ja|氏寺|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}).
== Career ==
In 689 (Jitō 3) Komaro petitioned for the manumission of 600 [[:en:Nobi|Nuhi]] (slaves), which was granted; he then held the cap rank {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Jitō 3, 10 / 22.</ref>
In 700 (Monmu 4) Emperor Monmu ordered Komaro, [[:en:Prince Osakabe|Prince Osakabe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], [[:en:Awata no Mahito|Awata no Mahito]] and others to revise the [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|ritsuryō codes]]; Komaro then held {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower).<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Monmu 4, 6 / 17.</ref>
With the promulgation of the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]] in 701 Komaro rose to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower. On 7 April he and two colleagues gave the first official lectures on the new code to princes and officials,<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 4 / 7.</ref> and by 3 August the code was completed.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Taihō 1, 8 / 3.</ref> For this achievement he was made [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|sangi]] in 702 and, in 703, received 30 chō of {{ill|meritorious fields|ja|功田|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}} and 50 fief households ({{ill|fuko|ja|封戸|1=fuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458391}}).
Thereafter he served concurrently as {{ill|Minister of War|ja|兵部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 兵部省|1=Ministry of the Military|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3276048}}, rising to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper. At Emperor Monmu’s death in 707 (Keiun 4) he was appointed {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supervisor of the imperial mausoleum, and secured imperial approval for {{ill|Shimotsuke no Ishishiro|ja|下毛野石代|1=Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361051}} to change the clan surname to Shimotsuke no Kawachi Ason.<ref>[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], Keiun 4, 3 / 22.</ref>
Under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]] he became {{ill|Minister of Civil Affairs|ja|式部卿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Ministry of Civil Services|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3727885}} and {{ill|Great General|ja|大将軍|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} in 708 (Wadō 1), being promoted to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower the same year. He died on 20 December 709 (Wadō 2). His last offices were Councillor, Minister of Civil Affairs and Great General. The sangi post then remained vacant until [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fujiwara no Fusasaki]] was appointed in 717 (Reiki 3).<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref>
== Offices and Ranks ==
(From the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]].)
* by 689 — {{ill|Jikikōshi|ja|直廣肆|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Junior Fifth Rank, Lower) * by 700 — {{ill|Jikikōsan|ja|直廣参|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (≈ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower) * 701 — Junior Fourth Rank, Lower 7 Apr 701 — {{ill|Udaiben|ja|右大弁|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (Right Greater Controller) * 21 May 702 — [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] * 15 Feb 703 — 10 chō meritorious fields 7 Mar 703 — 20 chō additional meritorious fields * 20 Apr 705 — Junior Fourth Rank, Upper<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 22 Apr 705 — Minister of War * 3 Oct 707 — {{ill|zō-ryō-shi|ja|造山陵司|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (imperial tomb works) * 13 Mar 708 — Minister of Civil Affairs 1 Jul 708 — Great General<ref>{{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任|1=Kugyō Bunin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391950}}</ref> 15 Jul 708 — Senior Fourth Rank, Lower * 20 Dec 709 — deceased (Councillor • Minister of Civil Affairs • Great General • Senior Fourth Rank, Lower)
== Miscellaneous ==
* In 2016 the north-[[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama Rotary Club]] produced a historical manga about Komaro.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 14 Apr 2016, p.30.</ref> In 2017 the manga was adapted into an animated video.<ref>''Yomiuri Shimbun'', Tochigi ed., 19 Feb 2017, p.34.</ref>
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Ujitani Mikoto|ja|宇治谷孟|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}. ''Dictionary of Ancient Clans and Personal Names of Ancient Japan''. {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1990.
